title: Upcoming federal food assistance pause intensifies shutdown fight
summary: Canned foods on grocery store shelves. (Photo by Cami Koons/Iowa Capital Dispatch) WASHINGTON — The stakes of the ongoing government shutdown rose Monday as the U.S. Department of Agriculture doubled down on its position that food benefits for November could not be paid and a union for federal workers implored lawmakers to pass a stopgap measure. As the government shutdown entered day 27, President Donald Trump’s administration sought to add pressure on U.S. Senate Democrats to approve the House Republicans’ stopgap government funding bill by refusing to use USDA resources to stretch critical food assistance benefits...

title: Nicholai Joekay
summary: Nicholai Joekay is a tribal citizen of the Native Village of Napaskiak in southwest Alaska and serves on the board of directors of Napaskiak, Incorporated. He resides in Bethel, Alaska, where he works as a public and government relations professional.

title: Alaska joins national trend in appointing state’s first Solicitor General
summary: Attorney General Stephen J. Cox has appointed Jenna Lorence as Alaska’s first State Solicitor General, a new centralized leadership position created to strengthen the state’s appellate advocacy and ensure consistent, high-quality representation in courts across the country. Lorence joins the Departm...

title: EDITORIAL: Alaska’s dismal state health gimmicks need old-school competition
summary: Over the past decade, Alaska has wasted hundreds of millions of dollars to promote better health. title: Tlingit & Haida expands Traditional Food Program with new grant
summary: A $25,000 award will help the Tribe upgrade equipment, fund harvests and strengthen food education.

title: Construction crew finds historical artifacts at Alder Top Village
summary: Construction is underway at Alder Top Village, a new subdivision in Wrangell. An Alaska Native boarding school sat on this property, beginning in the 1930s. It closed in 1975. Construction crews uncovered a shell midden in August, halting work near that particular site. (Colette Czarnecki/KSTK) Cons...

title: OPINION: Two Homer meetings reveal stark liberal/conservative divide
summary: I had the opportunity to participate in two meetings in Homer, held one week apart, which perfectly illustrate the current divide between liberals and conservatives. The way the meetings were conducted and how individuals participated says a lot about the political factions in this country. Ironical...

title: For America’s economy and health, Alaska seafood is the answer
summary: Alaska pollock, shown here from a harvest, make up the nation's top-volume single-species commercial seafood catch. Alaska pollock, harvested mostly in the Bering Sea but also in the Gulf of Alaska, are processed into fish sticks, fish burgers, imitation crab meat and other common fish products. (Photo provided by NOAA) As food prices rise and federal leaders revisit trade and nutrition policies, one solution has quietly been working for d...

title: Monday, October 27, 2025
summary: Photo: The Gila River Indian Community’s Sacaton Market was unveiled during its grand opening on October 23, 2025. (Gabriel Pietrorazio / KJZZ) The Gila River Indian Community in Arizona has long relied on convenience stores to feed its 13,000 residents, who otherwise may have to drive as far as half an hour away to neighboring cities with grocery stores. But as KJZZ’s Gabriel Pietrorazio reports, a new market – a decade i...

title: Refill ‘Er Up
summary: HOME | Page 17 Refill ‘Er Up Photo Credit: Christi Foist As recently as fifty years ago, milkmen still delivered door to door, and many people expected to return their used containers. All that changed with the rise of refrigeration, self-serve supermarkets, and lightweight plastic packaging. By 1975, just one in fifteen US consumers still put bottles on the stoop for the milkman to replace. That fraction has dwindled to nearly nothing these days, but a growing awareness of plastic’s downsides has helped create some fresh fans of reusable packaging and zero-waste retail. When Delta Junction dairy owner Scott Plagerman first decided to use glass bottles, he hoped the decision would reduce his long-term shipping costs and the need to regularly restock product packaging from Lower 48 suppliers. As with many aspects of business models that seek to reduce single-use packaging, the reality proved complex. Alaska has several retail stores with zero-waste grocery options, meaning they sell pro...

title: AST Criminal Mischief V: Fairbanks [AK25108717]
summary: On October 27, 2025, Alaska State Troopers responded to a report of a male subject bleeding from the mouth on a residential porch in Fairbanks. The individual, identified as Kyle David Sparks, appeared disoriented and under the influence of methamphetamine. Investigation revealed that Sparks had caused property damage and initiated a physical altercation, during which another individual acted in self-defense. No arrests were made at the time, and Sparks was transported to the hospital for treatment. Charges of Criminal Mischief in the Fifth Degree are being forwarded to the District Attorney’s Office for review.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Fail to Stop at Direction of a Peace Officer, Assault 2, DUI, VCOR.: Wasilla [AK25108559]
incident_type: Fail to Stop at Direction of a Peace Officer, Assault 2, DUI, VCOR.
summary: On 10-26-25 at 2122 hours AST received a report of a disturbance on W. Warbler Street. AST responded and observed one of the involved parties, Gabriel Lie-Schaeffer (32) leaving in a vehicle. A traffic stop was initiated, and Gabriel failed to yield. During the pursuit, he ignored traffic control devices, turned off his vehicle’s lights, and exceeded 100mph. He ultimately collided with another motorist on Clapp Road. The victim was taken to the hospital with broken bones. Gabriel was charged with Fail to Stop at Direction of a Peace Officer, Assault 2, DUI, and VCOR.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Fail to Stop at Direction of a Peace Officer, Assault 2, DUI, VCOR.
